mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market conditions and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.6% in Oct 2010 remains elevated post-crisis
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume indicates accelerating consumer demand and inventory replenishment cycles, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 5-trading-day window as market participants front-run earnings surprises.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 373103M in Oct 2010 reflects post-recession recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of near-zero federal funds rate with rising inflation expectations (measured by CPI) creates a negative real interest rate environment that compresses equity risk premiums and drives capital allocation toward equities, producing positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and CPIAUCSL at 218.372 in Sep-Oct 2010
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with emerging inflation pressures
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year window, or 60d forward returns in triggered regimes do not exceed non-triggered baseline by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and capital investment, which precedes earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a 20-trading-day horizon as supply-side momentum translates to profit margin expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 93.19 in Sep 2010 reflects post-recession industrial recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals weakening construction demand and reduced future consumer wealth effects, which depresses equity valuations through lower expected GDP growth and corporate earnings, producing negative 20-trading-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 610K in Sep 2010 remains depressed relative to pre-crisis levels
regime_hint: post-crisis demand weakness with housing market fragility
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of rising payroll employment with declining unemployment rate signals robust labor market tightening that increases wage pressures and corporate labor costs, which compresses profit margins and produces negative 60-day forward returns in a demand-constrained environment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth > 0.15% AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130462K and UNRATE at 9.6% in Oct 2010
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with wage inflation risks
failure_condition: 60d forward returns in triggered instances exceed non-triggered baseline by > 100 basis points (t-test p < 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth acceleration signals expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings potential, which drives positive equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon as market participants reprice growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.7B in Q2 2010 reflects post-recession recovery trajectory
regime_hint: early-to-mid cycle expansion with positive growth momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60d forward returns in growth-accelerating quarters do not exceed baseline by at least 200 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate combined with declining real retail sales growth signals monetary accommodation insufficient to overcome demand weakness, producing negative 5-trading-day forward returns as market participants recognize policy ineffectiveness and reprrice risk premiums upward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.19% and RSAFS at 373103M in Oct 2010 reflect policy accommodation amid demand uncertainty
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with persistent demand weakness and policy transmission breakdown
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.
